title: Why Oshkosh Stock Crashed Today
author: Rich Smith
description: Oshkosh Corporation stock plummeted 10.6% after reporting mixed Q1 2026 earnings. While the company beat sales expectations with $2.3B in revenue (up 0.2% YoY), it significantly missed on earnings with $0.85 per share (pro forma) versus expected $1.04, and actual GAAP earnings were only $0.68 per share, down 60% year-over-year. The company also reported negative free cash flow of $189.1M. Despite the disappointing results, management maintained full-year guidance of $10.90 per share GAAP earnings, suggesting solid demand visibility.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock crashed 10.6% due to significant earnings miss (60% YoY decline in GAAP profits), negative free cash flow of $189.1M, and minimal sales growth of only 0.2% YoY despite beating sales targets. While management maintained full-year guidance, the weak operational performance and cash burn indicate near-term challenges.

title: Did Polaris Just Turn Into a Drone Stock?
author: Rich Smith
description: Polaris beat Q1 2026 earnings expectations with strong revenue growth and margin expansion, but guided for flat sales through 2026 and reported a GAAP loss. The company's partnership with defense startup Forterra to manufacture autonomous ground vehicles (Mesa drone trucks) could reignite growth. While analysts raised price targets, most maintained neutral ratings, citing the need for stronger sales growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Beat earnings forecasts with 8% YoY sales growth and 420 basis point margin expansion. Partnership with Forterra on military autonomous vehicles presents new growth opportunity, though current guidance shows flat 2026 sales and GAAP losses remain a concern.
Mentioned as a partner with Forterra on autonomous ground vehicles, indicating exposure to growing defense autonomy market, but no specific company-level developments discussed.

title: Mine Disposal Vehicles Market Outlook Report 2026-2030 & 2035: Rising Defensive Needs Propel Market Expansion - Partnerships Boost Mine Disposal Vehicle Capabilities
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The global mine disposal vehicle market is projected to grow from $2.06 billion in 2025 to $3.27 billion by 2030, driven by rising defense budgets, technological innovations in autonomous systems, and increased humanitarian demining initiatives. Key developments include Advanced Blast & Ballistic Systems' Dragon UGV and a strategic partnership between Rheinmetall AG and DOK-ING to develop advanced unmanned disposal systems. North America leads the market while Asia-Pacific shows the fastest growth potential.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a major market participant in the growing mine disposal vehicle market with strong market positioning and expected growth through 2030.
Identified as a major market participant in the expanding mine disposal vehicle market with strong growth projections through 2030.
Actively developing cutting-edge unmanned disposal systems through strategic partnership with DOK-ING, demonstrating innovation and market engagement in a growing sector.

title: 557,006 OshKosh (OSK) Shares Worth $63.2 Million Sold by SG Capital
author: Cory Renauer
description: SG Capital Management LLC completely exited its stake in Oshkosh Corporation, selling all 557,006 shares in the third quarter of 2025. The sale represents a significant portfolio change, with the company previously holding OSK as its second-largest holding.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock has slightly underperformed the market (8.2% gain vs S&P 500's 12.7%), and the company adjusted its earnings outlook, indicating some economic uncertainty
Mentioned as one of SG Capital's top holdings after OSK exit, with no specific performance details provided

title: Forterra Unveils Next-Generation Integrated Mission Modules for Strengthened Autonomous and Connected Operations
author: Joseph Putney (Cto)
description: Forterra introduced four integrated autonomous mission modules (AutoDrive, TerraLink, OASIS, and Vektor) designed to enhance military logistics, mobility, and communication capabilities through advanced data processing and interoperability technologies.
